---
title: "Meeting_03_10_2025"
author: "Matthew"
output: github_document
---

# Purpose

This document summarises the empirical methods employed in Heckman (2006)

# Structural modelling of Heckman (2006)

[Here is the web appendix to the original Heckman paper.](https://jenni.uchicago.edu/noncog/web_supplement.pdf) Still need to read through this more comprehensively


Notes taken from __Section VI (Implementing the Model)__ of Heckman paper:

- Use Bayesian Markov chain Monte Carlo methods to compute sample likelihood
- Modelled as outcome variables:
    - Log hourly wages at age 30
    - Employment
    - Occupational choice
    - Educational choice
    - Behavioural outcomes
    - Work experience
    - Fertility choice
- Multinomial probit model used for educational choice model
- Wage equations at age 30 estimated for individuals at each final schooling level
- Dummies included:
    - Race
    - Cohort (Age/Year of birth)
    - Local labour market conditions (check Cameron and Heckman (2001) for list of variables used)
    - Region of residence
- Distributions of the unobservables identified nonparametrically

- Last figures in the paper are generated from data from simulations of estimates from models and sample data
    
_Things that confused me_:

- footnote 16:
    - "analysis in Carneiro (2003), Hansen (2004) and Heckman and Navarro (2006) establishes conditions on the support of the regressors that allow for semiparametric identification of the model"
    - "for the support conditions for semiparametric identification to hold, the support of the distribution for each probability should be the unit interval [0,1]"
    
    ```{r echo=FALSE, out.width='50%'}
    knitr::include_graphics('/Users/matthewthompson/Documents/Stellenbosch University/Masters/Research Assignment/Data_work/output/Image 2025-10-02 at 18.51.jpeg')
    ```
    
    - "Evident that this condition is not met, although condition for 4-year college graduation nearly satisfied"
    - "This evidence suggests empirical results that are generated are identified from the parametric structure of the model"
    - "However, use a robust mixture of normal approximation to the underlying distributions"
    
- 2 normalisations required to assure identification of model:
    - Set the scale of factors
    - Normalise the loadings $(\alpha_{C_i}(s_T), \alpha_{N_j}(s'_T))$ of cognitive and noncognitive factors to be $=1$ in equations associated with coding speed and Self-esteem scale for individuals in grades 9-11 at time of test

- GHK (Geweke, Hajivassiliou and Keane) algorithm
    - used to recover probabilities from mutlivariate probit
    - can specify this argument in the `mvProbit` function
